info.name: Shell Game
info.attribution: Layout, scripting, and library image by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and LawDawg and released to the Public Domain under CC0.
info.description: Place tiles in sequences or sets to score points and maximize control of each of the four areas on the board.
info.helpText:
Players sit in seats. Press the Setup button to begin.

Other usability information is found at the bottom of the screen by hovering over the information boxes.

basicAid.detail:
Set Up

Press the "Setup" button to prepare the board automatically for the current player count and randomly choose the starting player.

Play Actions

On your turn, either: place, claim, or steal to add one of your tokens to the board.

Place a numbered green tile from your rack onto any empty space on the board. Add a token to the tile by double clicking or dragging it.

Draw back up to 5 tiles. You can draw from the bag or the display of tiles at the bottom of the board.

Dark spaces: If you place a tile adjacent to an empty dark space, take a random tile from the bag and put it on that space.

Claim an available tile when the total strength of your adjacent tiles is higher than any other player’s adjacent strength. Put one of your tokens on it by double clicking the tile or by dragging a token.

Steal an opponent's tile when the strength of your adjacent tiles is greater than the strength of that tile plus all of that opponent’s adjacent tiles. Do this by double clicking the tile. The tile gets turned face down, which is wild, has no strength, and cannot be stolen again.

At the end of your turn, press the "Current Turn" button to move to the next player. This will refill your hand (from the bag) and the tile display at the bottom.

Special Tiles

Two (2): When you place or claim one of these tiles, take one additional action right away. This bonus can happen only once during your turn. Wait until after your second action to refill your hand.

One (1): For claiming and stealing, these tiles count as strength 10.

Seven (7) and Eight (8): For claiming and stealing, these tiles count as strength 0.

Refresh: Before or after your main action and one time per game, you may use your refresh tile. To use it, drag tiles you want to replace from your hand and/or the display at the bottom of the board to the holder next to the history list. Then click the "Refresh" button that appears once cards are in the holder.

End of Game

When a player places their final token on a tile, finish the current round so every player gets the same number of turns. The game still ends after that round even if the player later gets a token back because of a steal.

scoreAid.detail:
Scoring During Actions

Moving score markers and 50 point markers is automated if you click the "vp" area next to your seat and enter the value to add or subtract. You can also manually move markers and the "vp" area will automatically be updated.

Whenever a player places or claims a tile, score only the tile just added or claimed, not the rest of the group. A group is 3 or more connected tiles owned by the same player and can be either a sequence or a set.

Sequence: A connected group with tile values in number order. When your token is added to a tile that is part of a sequence, gain points equal to that tile’s value. A tile can belong to more than one sequence, but its value is scored only once.

Set: A connected group where all tiles show the same value. When your token is added to a tile in a set, gain one point for each tile in that set, including the tile just marked.

End of Game Scoring

Manually take all tiles not part of a group off the board. Deduct points for each player's tiles removed equal to the value of the tiles. Only subtract 1 point for a 1 tile (not 10) and 0 for wild/upside down tiles.

Then click the "End Game Score" button to automatically calculate the final scores. The calculation follows the guidelines below.

Three or Four Players: In each of the four quadrants on the board, players earn points according to who controls the most tiles there. A player must have at least one tile in the quadrant to score. If players tie, the points for the tied ranks are added and split between the players, rounding down.

  • 1st: 20 points
  • 2nd: 16 points
  • 3rd: 10 points
  • 4th: 8 points

Two Players: In each quadrant, scoring depends on which player has more tiles and how many tiles the other player has there.

First place: 10 points

Second place:

  • -10 points for 0
  • -5 points for 1-2 tiles
  • -2 points for 3 tiles
  • 0 points for 4 or more tiles)
